serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of a Paneer Saagwala set meal, including palak paneer, dal tadka, saffron pulao, and butter naan, is approximately 600 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Food Composition Guide
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are estimated by combining data for individual components (Palak Paneer, Dal Tadka, Saffron Pulao, Butter Naan) from HPB Singapore Foodo, scaled to a typical set meal portion.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database (components)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index estimated from the weighted average of key components: Naan (GI 70), Basmati Rice (GI 55), Lentil Dal (GI 29), and Paneer (very low GI), based on data from the University of Sydney GI Database.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should monitor portion sizes due to the moderate sodium content (approximately 252 mg per 100 g), particularly from the dal and naan components.

type2_diabetes.guidance: Due to its medium glycemic index (GI 62) and moderate carbohydrate content, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this meal in controlled portions and consider pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While a vegetarian dish, the presence of paneer and butter naan contributes to moderate saturated fat (approximately 2.9 g per 100 g), which individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume in moderation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Paneer Saagwala Set is a popular Indian vegetarian meal featuring a rich and creamy spinach and paneer (Indian cheese) curry, known as Palak Paneer. This set typically includes a serving of Dal Tadka, a flavorful lentil dish tempered with spices, aromatic Saffron Pulao (basmati rice cooked with saffron), and soft, leavened Butter Naan bread. The Palak Paneer offers a mild, earthy taste with a smooth texture from the spinach puree and tender paneer cubes. Dal Tadka provides a comforting, savory, and slightly spicy counterpoint, while the pulao adds a fragrant, fluffy rice base. The Butter Naan, often brushed with ghee, is perfect for scooping up the curries. Nutritionally, this set is a good source of protein and carbohydrates, with moderate fat and sodium, and a medium glycemic index, making it a substantial and satisfying meal. It contains dairy and gluten and is suitable for vegetarians. Clinically, the moderate GI and sodium content require attention for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease.
